NKJV.1: A Psalm of Asaph. Truly God is good to Israel, To such as are pure in heart.
NKJV.2: But as for me, my feet had almost stumbled; My steps had nearly slipped.
NKJV.3: For I was envious of the boastful, When I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
NKJV.4: For there are no pangs in their death, But their strength is firm.
NKJV.5: They are not in trouble as other men, Nor are they plagued like other men.
NKJV.6: Therefore pride serves as their necklace; Violence covers them like a garment.
NKJV.7: Their eyes bulge with abundance; They have more than heart could wish.
NKJV.8: They scoff and speak wickedly concerning oppression; They speak loftily.
NKJV.9: They set their mouth against the heavens, And their tongue walks through the earth.
NKJV.10: Therefore his people return here, And waters of a full cup are drained by them.
NKJV.11: And they say, “How does God know? And is there knowledge in the Most High?”
NKJV.12: Behold, these are the ungodly, Who are always at ease; They increase in riches.
NKJV.13: Surely I have cleansed my heart in vain, And washed my hands in innocence.
NKJV.14: For all day long I have been plagued, And chastened every morning.
NKJV.15: If I had said, “I will speak thus,” Behold, I would have been untrue to the generation of Your children.
NKJV.16: When I thought how to understand this, It was too painful for me—
NKJV.17: Until I went into the sanctuary of God; Then I understood their end.
NKJV.18: Surely You set them in slippery places; You cast them down to destruction.
NKJV.19: Oh, how they are brought to desolation, as in a moment! They are utterly consumed with terrors.
NKJV.20: As a dream when one awakes, So, Lord, when You awake, You shall despise their image.
NKJV.21: Thus my heart was grieved, And I was vexed in my mind.
NKJV.22: I was so foolish and ignorant; I was like a beast before You.
NKJV.23: Nevertheless I am continually with You; You hold me by my right hand.
NKJV.24: You will guide me with Your counsel, And afterward receive me to glory.
NKJV.25: Whom have I in heaven but You? And there is none upon earth that I desire besides You.
NKJV.26: My flesh and my heart fail; But God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
NKJV.27: For indeed, those who are far from You shall perish; You have destroyed all those who desert You for harlotry.
NKJV.28: But it is good for me to draw near to God; I have put my trust in the Lord GOD, That I may declare all Your works.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. Truly God<S>430</S> is good<S>2896</S> to Israel<S>3478</S>, even to such as are of a clean<S>1249</S> heart<S>3824</S>. <sup>of: or, for</sup> <sup>Truly: or, Yet</sup> <sup>of: Heb. clean of heart</sup>
KJV.2: But as for me, my feet<S>7272</S> were almost<S>4592</S> gone<S>5186</S> <S>5186</S>; my steps<S>838</S> had well nigh<S>369</S> slipped<S>8210</S>.
KJV.3: For I was envious<S>7065</S> at the foolish<S>1984</S>, when I saw<S>7200</S> the prosperity<S>7965</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>.

KJV.6: Therefore pride<S>1346</S> compasseth them about as a chain<S>6059</S>; violence<S>2555</S> covereth<S>5848</S> them as a garment<S>7897</S>.
KJV.7: Their eyes<S>5869</S> stand out<S>3318</S> with fatness<S>2459</S>: they have more<S>5674</S> than heart<S>3824</S> could wish<S>4906</S>. <sup>have: Heb. pass the thoughts of the heart</sup>
KJV.8: They are corrupt<S>4167</S>, and speak<S>1696</S> wickedly<S>7451</S> concerning oppression<S>6233</S>: they speak<S>1696</S> loftily<S>4791</S>.
KJV.9: They set<S>8371</S> their mouth<S>6310</S> against the heavens<S>8064</S>, and their tongue<S>3956</S> walketh<S>1980</S> through the earth<S>776</S>.

KJV.11: And they say<S>559</S>, How doth God<S>410</S> know<S>3045</S>? and is there<S>3426</S> knowledge<S>1844</S> in the most High<S>5945</S>?
KJV.12: Behold, these are the ungodly<S>7563</S>, who prosper<S>7961</S> in the world<S>5769</S>; they increase<S>7685</S> in riches<S>2428</S>.
KJV.13: Verily I have cleansed<S>2135</S> my heart<S>3824</S> in vain<S>7385</S>, and washed<S>7364</S> my hands<S>3709</S> in innocency<S>5356</S>.
KJV.14: For all the day<S>3117</S> long have I been plagued<S>5060</S>, and chastened<S>8433</S> every morning<S>1242</S>. <sup>chastened: Heb. my chastisement was</sup>
KJV.15: If I say<S>559</S>, I will speak<S>5608</S> thus<S>3644</S>; behold, I should offend<S>898</S> against the generation<S>1755</S> of thy children<S>1121</S>.
KJV.16: When I thought<S>2803</S> to know<S>3045</S> this, it was too painful<S>5999</S> for me<S>5869</S>; <sup>too: Heb. labour in mine eyes</sup>
KJV.17: Until I went<S>935</S> into the sanctuary<S>4720</S> of God<S>410</S>; then understood<S>995</S> I their end<S>319</S>.
KJV.18: Surely thou didst set<S>7896</S> them in slippery places<S>2513</S>: thou castedst them down<S>5307</S> into destruction<S>4876</S>.
KJV.19: How are they brought into desolation<S>8047</S>, as in a moment<S>7281</S>! they are utterly<S>5486</S> consumed<S>8552</S> with terrors<S>1091</S>.
KJV.20: As a dream<S>2472</S> when one awaketh<S>6974</S>; so, O Lord<S>136</S>, when thou awakest<S>5782</S>, thou shalt despise<S>959</S> their image<S>6754</S>.

KJV.23: Nevertheless I am continually<S>8548</S> with thee: thou hast holden<S>270</S> me by my right<S>3225</S> hand<S>3027</S>.

KJV.25: Whom have I in heaven<S>8064</S> but thee? and there is none upon earth<S>776</S> that I desire<S>2654</S> beside thee.
KJV.26: My flesh<S>7607</S> and my heart<S>3824</S> faileth<S>3615</S>: but God<S>430</S> is the strength<S>6697</S> of my heart<S>3824</S>, and my portion<S>2506</S> for ever<S>5769</S>. <sup>strength: Heb. rock</sup>
KJV.27: For, lo, they that are far<S>7369</S> from thee shall perish<S>6</S>: thou hast destroyed<S>6789</S> all them that go a whoring<S>2181</S> from thee.
KJV.28: But<S>589</S> it is good<S>2896</S> for me to draw near<S>7132</S> to God<S>430</S>: I have put<S>7896</S> my trust<S>4268</S> in the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>, that I may declare<S>5608</S> all thy works<S>4399</S>.

ESV.1: Truly God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
ESV.2: But as for me, my feet had almost stumbled, my steps had nearly slipped.
ESV.3: For I was envious of the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
ESV.4: For they have no pangs until death; their bodies are fat and sleek.
ESV.5: They are not in trouble as others are; they are not stricken like the rest of mankind.
ESV.6: Therefore pride is their necklace; violence covers them as a garment.
ESV.7: Their eyes swell out through fatness; their hearts overflow with follies.
ESV.8: They scoff and speak with malice; loftily they threaten oppression.
ESV.9: They set their mouths against the heavens, and their tongue struts through the earth.
ESV.10: Therefore his people turn back to them, and find no fault in them.
ESV.11: And they say, “How can God know? Is there knowledge in the Most High?”
ESV.12: Behold, these are the wicked; always at ease, they increase in riches.
ESV.13: All in vain have I kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence.
ESV.14: For all the day long I have been stricken and rebuked every morning.
ESV.15: If I had said, “I will speak thus,” I would have betrayed the generation of your children.
ESV.16: But when I thought how to understand this, it seemed to me a wearisome task,
ESV.17: until I went into the sanctuary of God; then I discerned their end.
ESV.18: Truly you set them in slippery places; you make them fall to ruin.
ESV.19: How they are destroyed in a moment, swept away utterly by terrors!
ESV.20: Like a dream when one awakes, O Lord, when you rouse yourself, you despise them as phantoms.
ESV.21: When my soul was embittered, when I was pricked in heart,
ESV.22: I was brutish and ignorant; I was like a beast toward you.
ESV.23: Nevertheless, I am continually with you; you hold my right hand.
ESV.24: You guide me with your counsel, and afterward you will receive me to glory.
ESV.25: Whom have I in heaven but you? And there is nothing on earth that I desire besides you.
ESV.26: My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength  of my heart and my portion forever.
ESV.27: For behold, those who are far from you shall perish; you put an end to everyone who is unfaithful to you.
ESV.28: But for me it is good to be near God; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, that I may tell of all your works.

NASB.1: A Psalm of Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, To those who are pure in heart!
NASB.2: But as for me, my feet came close to stumbling, My steps had almost slipped.
NASB.3: For I was envious of the arrogant [As] I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
NASB.4: For there are no pains in their death, And their body is fat.
NASB.5: They are not in trouble [as other] men, Nor are they plagued like mankind.
NASB.6: Therefore pride is their necklace; The garment of violence covers them.
NASB.7: Their eye bulges from fatness; The imaginations of [their] heart run riot.
NASB.8: They mock and wickedly speak of oppression; They speak from on high.
NASB.9: They have set their mouth against the heavens, And their tongue parades through the earth.
NASB.10: Therefore his people return to this place, And waters of abundance are drunk by them.
NASB.11: They say, 'How does God know? And is there knowledge with the Most High?'
NASB.12: Behold, these are the wicked; And always at ease, they have increased [in] wealth.
NASB.13: Surely in vain I have kept my heart pure And washed my hands in innocence;
NASB.14: For I have been stricken all day long And chastened every morning.
NASB.15: If I had said, 'I will speak thus,' Behold, I would have betrayed the generation of Your children.
NASB.16: When I pondered to understand this, It was troublesome in my sight
NASB.17: Until I came into the sanctuary of God; [Then] I perceived their end.
NASB.18: Surely You set them in slippery places; You cast them down to destruction.
NASB.19: How they are destroyed in a moment! They are utterly swept away by sudden terrors!
NASB.20: Like a dream when one awakes, O Lord, when aroused, You will despise their form.
NASB.21: When my heart was embittered And I was pierced within,
NASB.22: Then I was senseless and ignorant; I was [like] a beast before You.
NASB.23: Nevertheless I am continually with You; You have taken hold of my right hand.
NASB.24: With Your counsel You will guide me, And afterward receive me to glory.
NASB.25: Whom have I in heaven [but You]? And besides You, I desire nothing on earth.
NASB.26: My flesh and my heart may fail, But God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
NASB.27: For, behold, those who are far from You will perish; You have destroyed all those who are unfaithful to You.
NASB.28: But as for me, the nearness of God is my good; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, That I may tell of all Your works.

NLT.1:
Truly God is good to Israel,
to those whose hearts are pure.
NLT.2:
But as for me, I almost lost my footing.
My feet were slipping, and I was almost gone.
NLT.3:
For I envied the proud
when I saw them prosper despite their wickedness.
NLT.4:
They seem to live such painless lives;
their bodies are so healthy and strong.
NLT.5:
They don’t have troubles like other people;
they’re not plagued with problems like everyone else.
NLT.6:
They wear pride like a jeweled necklace
and clothe themselves with cruelty.
NLT.7:
These fat cats have everything
their hearts could ever wish for!
NLT.8:
They scoff and speak only evil;
in their pride they seek to crush others.
NLT.9:
They boast against the very heavens,
and their words strut throughout the earth.
NLT.10:
And so the people are dismayed and confused,
drinking in all their words.
NLT.11:
“What does God know?” they ask.
“Does the Most High even know what’s happening?”
NLT.12:
Look at these wicked people —
enjoying a life of ease while their riches multiply.
NLT.13:
Did I keep my heart pure for nothing?
Did I keep myself innocent for no reason?
NLT.14:
I get nothing but trouble all day long;
every morning brings me pain.
NLT.15:
If I had really spoken this way to others,
I would have been a traitor to your people.
NLT.16:
So I tried to understand why the wicked prosper.
But what a difficult task it is!
NLT.17:
Then I went into your sanctuary, O God,
and I finally understood the destiny of the wicked.
NLT.18:
Truly, you put them on a slippery path
and send them sliding over the cliff to destruction.
NLT.19:
In an instant they are destroyed,
completely swept away by terrors.
NLT.20:
When you arise, O Lord,
you will laugh at their silly ideas
as a person laughs at dreams in the morning.
NLT.21:
Then I realized that my heart was bitter,
and I was all torn up inside.
NLT.22:
I was so foolish and ignorant —
I must have seemed like a senseless animal to you.
NLT.23:
Yet I still belong to you;
you hold my right hand.
NLT.24:
You guide me with your counsel,
leading me to a glorious destiny.
NLT.25:
Whom have I in heaven but you?
I desire you more than anything on earth.
NLT.26:
My health may fail, and my spirit may grow weak,
but God remains the strength of my heart;
he is mine forever.
NLT.27:
Those who desert him will perish,
for you destroy those who abandon you.
NLT.28:
But as for me, how good it is to be near God!
I have made the Sovereign LORD my shelter,
and I will tell everyone about the wonderful things you do.

WEB.1: A Psalm by Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
WEB.2: But as for me, my feet were almost gone. My steps had nearly slipped.
WEB.3: For I was envious of the arrogant, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
WEB.4: For there are no struggles in their death, but their strength is firm.
WEB.5: They are free from burdens of men, neither are they plagued like other men.
WEB.6: Therefore pride is like a chain around their neck. Violence covers them like a garment.
WEB.7: Their eyes bulge with fat. Their minds pass the limits of conceit.
WEB.8: They scoff and speak with malice. In arrogance, they threaten oppression.
WEB.9: They have set their mouth in the heavens. Their tongue walks through the earth.
WEB.10: Therefore their people return to them, and they drink up waters of abundance.
WEB.11: They say, “How does God know? Is there knowledge in the Most High?”
WEB.12: Behold, these are the wicked. Being always at ease, they increase in riches.
WEB.13: Surely I have cleansed my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocence,
WEB.14: For all day long I have been plagued, and punished every morning.
WEB.15: If I had said, “I will speak thus”; behold, I would have betrayed the generation of your children.
WEB.16: When I tried to understand this, it was too painful for me;
WEB.17: Until I entered God’s sanctuary, and considered their latter end.
WEB.18: Surely you set them in slippery places. You throw them down to destruction.
WEB.19: How they are suddenly destroyed! They are completely swept away with terrors.
WEB.20: As a dream when one wakes up, so, Lord,  when you awake, you will despise their fantasies.
WEB.21: For my soul was grieved. I was embittered in my heart.
WEB.22: I was so senseless and ignorant. I was a brute beast before you.
WEB.23: Nevertheless, I am continually with you. You have held my right hand.
WEB.24: You will guide me with your counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.
WEB.25: Whom do I have in heaven? There is no one on earth whom I desire besides you.
WEB.26: My flesh and my heart fails, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
WEB.27: For, behold, those who are far from you shall perish. You have destroyed all those who are unfaithful to you.
WEB.28: But it is good for me to come close to God. I have made the Lord Yahweh  my refuge, that I may tell of all your works.

CSB17.1:
A psalm of Asaph. ⓜ [1] God is indeed good to Israel,
to the pure in heart. ⓝ
CSB17.2:
But as for me, my feet almost slipped;
my steps nearly went astray. ⓐ
CSB17.3:
For I envied the arrogant;
I saw the prosperity of the wicked. ⓑ
CSB17.4:
They have an easy time until they die, [1]
and their bodies are well fed. [2] ⓒ
CSB17.5:
They are not in trouble like others;
they are not afflicted like most people. ⓓ
CSB17.6:
Therefore, pride is their necklace,
and violence covers them like a garment. ⓔ
CSB17.7:
Their eyes bulge out from fatness;
the imaginations of their hearts run wild. ⓕ
CSB17.8:
They mock, and they speak maliciously;
they arrogantly threaten oppression. ⓖ
CSB17.9:
They set their mouths against heaven,
and their tongues strut across the earth. ⓗ
CSB17.10:
Therefore his people turn to them [3]
and drink in their overflowing words. [4] ⓘ
CSB17.11:
The wicked say, “How can God know?
Does the Most High know everything?” ⓙ
CSB17.12:
Look at them — the wicked!
They are always at ease,
and they increase their wealth. ⓚ
CSB17.13:
Did I purify my heart
and wash my hands in innocence for nothing? ⓛ
CSB17.14:
For I am afflicted all day long
and punished every morning. ⓜ
CSB17.15:
If I had decided to say these things aloud,
I would have betrayed your people. [5] ⓝ
CSB17.16:
When I tried to understand all this,
it seemed hopeless [6] ⓐ
CSB17.17:
until I entered God’s sanctuary.
Then I understood their destiny. ⓑ
CSB17.18:
Indeed, you put them in slippery places;
you make them fall into ruin. ⓒ
CSB17.19:
How suddenly they become a desolation!
They come to an end, swept away by terrors. ⓓ
CSB17.20:
Like one waking from a dream,
Lord, when arising, you will despise their image. ⓔ
CSB17.21:
When I became embittered
and my innermost being [7] was wounded, ⓕ
CSB17.22:
I was stupid and didn’t understand;
I was an unthinking animal toward you. ⓖ
CSB17.23:
Yet I am always with you;
you hold my right hand. ⓗ
CSB17.24:
You guide me with your counsel,
and afterward you will take me up in glory. [8] ⓘ
CSB17.25:
Who do I have in heaven but you?
And I desire nothing on earth but you. ⓙ
CSB17.26:
My flesh and my heart may fail,
but God is the strength [9] of my heart,
my portion forever. ⓚ
CSB17.27:
Those far from you will certainly perish;
you destroy all who are unfaithful to you. ⓛ
CSB17.28:
But as for me, God’s presence is my good.
I have made the Lord GOD my refuge,
so I can tell about all you do. ⓜ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of Asaph. Truly God is good to Israel,
To those who are pure in heart.
AMP.2:
But as for me, my feet came close to stumbling,
My steps had almost slipped.
AMP.3:
For I was envious of the arrogant
As I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
AMP.4:
For there are no pains in their death,
Their body is fat and pampered.
AMP.5:
They are not in trouble as other men,
Nor are they plagued like mankind.
AMP.6:
Therefore pride is their necklace;
Violence covers them like a garment [like a long, luxurious robe].
AMP.7:
Their eye bulges from fatness [they have more than the heart desires];
The imaginations of their mind run riot [with foolishness].
AMP.8:
They mock and wickedly speak of oppression;
They speak loftily [with malice].
AMP.9:
They set their mouth against the heavens,
And their tongue swaggers through the earth.
AMP.10:
Therefore his people return to this place,
And waters of abundance [offered by the irreverent] are [blindly] drunk by them.
AMP.11:
They say, "How does God know?
Is there knowledge [of us] with the Most High?"
AMP.12:
Behold, these are the ungodly,
Who always prosper and are at ease [in the world]; they have increased in wealth.
AMP.13:
Surely then in vain I have cleansed my heart
And washed my hands in innocence.
AMP.14:
For all the day long have I been stricken,
And punished every morning.
AMP.15:
If I had said, "I will say this," [and expressed my feelings],
I would have betrayed the generation of Your children.
AMP.16:
When I considered how to understand this,
It was too great an effort for me and too painful
AMP.17:
Until I came into the sanctuary of God;
Then I understood [for I considered] their end.
AMP.18:
Surely You set the wicked-minded and immoral on slippery places;
You cast them down to destruction.
AMP.19:
How they are destroyed in a moment!
They are completely swept away by sudden terrors!
AMP.20:
Like a dream [which seems real] until one awakens,
O Lord, when stirred, [You observe the wicked], You will despise their image.
AMP.21:
When my heart was embittered
And I was pierced within [as with the fang of an adder],
AMP.22:
Then I was senseless and ignorant;
I was like a beast before You.
AMP.23:
Nevertheless I am continually with You;
You have taken hold of my right hand.
AMP.24:
You will guide me with Your counsel,
And afterward receive me to honor and glory.
AMP.25:
Whom have I in heaven [but You]?
And besides You, I desire nothing on earth.
AMP.26:
My flesh and my heart may fail,
But God is the rock and strength of my heart and my portion forever.
AMP.27:
For behold, those who are far from You will perish;
You have destroyed all those who are unfaithful and have abandoned You.
AMP.28:
But as for me, it is good for me to draw near to God;
I have made the Lord GOD my refuge and placed my trust in Him,
That I may tell of all Your works.

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
No doubt about it! God is good—
good to good people, good to the good-hearted.
MSG.2:
But I nearly missed it,
missed seeing his goodness.
MSG.3:
I was looking the other way,
looking up to the people
MSG.4:
At the top,
envying the wicked who have it made,
MSG.5:
Who have nothing to worry about,
not a care in the whole wide world.
MSG.6:
Pretentious with arrogance,
they wear the latest fashions in violence,
MSG.7:
Pampered and overfed,
decked out in silk bows of silliness.
MSG.8:
They jeer, using words to kill;
they bully their way with words.
MSG.9:
They're full of hot air,
loudmouths disturbing the peace.
MSG.10:
People actually listen to them—can you believe it?
Like thirsty puppies, they lap up their words.
MSG.11:
What's going on here? Is God out to lunch?
Nobody's tending the store.
MSG.12:
The wicked get by with everything;
they have it made, piling up riches
MSG.13:
I've been stupid to play by the rules;
what has it gotten me?
MSG.14:
A long run of bad luck, that's what—
a slap in the face every time I walk out the door.
MSG.15:
If I'd have given in and talked like this,
I would have betrayed your dear children.
MSG.16:
Still, when I tried to figure it out,
all I got was a splitting headache...
MSG.17:
Until I entered the sanctuary of God.
Then I saw the whole picture:
MSG.18:
The slippery road you've put them on,
with a final crash in a ditch of delusions.
MSG.19:
In the blink of an eye, disaster!
A blind curve in the dark, and—nightmare!
MSG.20:
We wake up and rub our eyes... . Nothing.
There's nothing to them. And there never was.
MSG.21:
When I was beleaguered and bitter,
totally consumed by envy,
MSG.22:
I was totally ignorant, a dumb ox
in your very presence.
MSG.23:
I'm still in your presence,
but you've taken my hand.
MSG.24:
You wisely and tenderly lead me,
and then you bless me.
MSG.25:
You're all I want in heaven!
You're all I want on earth!
MSG.26:
When my skin sags and my bones get brittle,
God is rock-firm and faithful.
MSG.27:
Look! Those who left you are falling apart!
Deserters, they'll never be heard from again.
MSG.28:
But I'm in the very presence of God—
oh, how refreshing it is!
I've made Lord God my home.
God, I'm telling the world what you do!

NIV.1:
BOOK III
Psalms 73–89
Psalm 73
A psalm of Asaph.
Surely God is good to Israel,
to those who are pure in heart.
NIV.2:
But as for me, my feet had almost slipped;
I had nearly lost my foothold.
NIV.3:
For I envied the arrogant
when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
NIV.4:
They have no struggles;
their bodies are healthy and strong.
NIV.5:
They are free from the burdens common to man;
they are not plagued by human ills.
NIV.6:
Therefore pride is their necklace;
they clothe themselves with violence.
NIV.7:
From their callous hearts comes iniquity;
the evil conceits of their minds know no limits.
NIV.8:
They scoff, and speak with malice;
in their arrogance they threaten oppression.
NIV.9:
Their mouths lay claim to heaven,
and their tongues take possession of the earth.
NIV.10:
Therefore their people turn to them
and drink up waters in abundance.
NIV.11:
They say, “How can God know?
Does the Most High have knowledge?”
NIV.12:
This is what the wicked are like—
always carefree, they increase in wealth.
NIV.13:
Surely in vain have I kept my heart pure;
in vain have I washed my hands in innocence.
NIV.14:
All day long I have been plagued;
I have been punished every morning.
NIV.15:
If I had said, “I will speak thus,”
I would have betrayed your children.
NIV.16:
When I tried to understand all this,
it was oppressive to me
NIV.17:
till I entered the sanctuary of God;
then I understood their final destiny.
NIV.18:
Surely you place them on slippery ground;
you cast them down to ruin.
NIV.19:
How suddenly are they destroyed,
completely swept away by terrors!
NIV.20:
As a dream when one awakes,
so when you arise, O Lord,
you will despise them as fantasies.
NIV.21:
When my heart was grieved
and my spirit embittered,
NIV.22:
I was senseless and ignorant;
I was a brute beast before you.
NIV.23:
Yet I am always with you;
you hold me by my right hand.
NIV.24:
You guide me with your counsel,
and afterward you will take me into glory.
NIV.25:
Whom have I in heaven but you?
And earth has nothing I desire besides you.
NIV.26:
My flesh and my heart may fail,
but God is the strength of my heart
and my portion forever.
NIV.27:
Those who are far from you will perish;
you destroy all who are unfaithful to you.
NIV.28:
But as for me, it is good to be near God.
I have made the Sovereign Lord my refuge;
I will tell of all your deeds.

NRSVCE.1:
Truly God is good to the upright,
to those who are pure in heart.
NRSVCE.2:
But as for me, my feet had almost stumbled;
my steps had nearly slipped.
NRSVCE.3:
For I was envious of the arrogant;
I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
NRSVCE.4:
For they have no pain;
their bodies are sound and sleek.
NRSVCE.5:
They are not in trouble as others are;
they are not plagued like other people.
NRSVCE.6:
Therefore pride is their necklace;
violence covers them like a garment.
NRSVCE.7:
Their eyes swell out with fatness;
their hearts overflow with follies.
NRSVCE.8:
They scoff and speak with malice;
loftily they threaten oppression.
NRSVCE.9:
They set their mouths against heaven,
and their tongues range over the earth.
NRSVCE.10:
Therefore the people turn and praise them,
and find no fault in them.
NRSVCE.11:
And they say, “How can God know?
Is there knowledge in the Most High?”
NRSVCE.12:
Such are the wicked;
always at ease, they increase in riches.
NRSVCE.13:
All in vain I have kept my heart clean
and washed my hands in innocence.
NRSVCE.14:
For all day long I have been plagued,
and am punished every morning.
NRSVCE.15:
If I had said, “I will talk on in this way,”
I would have been untrue to the circle of your children.
NRSVCE.16:
But when I thought how to understand this,
it seemed to me a wearisome task,
NRSVCE.17:
until I went into the sanctuary of God;
then I perceived their end.
NRSVCE.18:
Truly you set them in slippery places;
you make them fall to ruin.
NRSVCE.19:
How they are destroyed in a moment,
swept away utterly by terrors!
NRSVCE.20:
They are like a dream when one awakes;
on awaking you despise their phantoms.
NRSVCE.21:
When my soul was embittered,
when I was pricked in heart,
NRSVCE.22:
I was stupid and ignorant;
I was like a brute beast toward you.
NRSVCE.23:
Nevertheless I am continually with you;
you hold my right hand.
NRSVCE.24:
You guide me with your counsel,
and afterward you will receive me with honor.
NRSVCE.25:
Whom have I in heaven but you?
And there is nothing on earth that I desire other than you.
NRSVCE.26:
My flesh and my heart may fail,
but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
NRSVCE.27:
Indeed, those who are far from you will perish;
you put an end to those who are false to you.
NRSVCE.28:
But for me it is good to be near God;
I have made the Lord GOD my refuge,
to tell of all your works.

NET.1: Certainly God is good to Israel, and to those whose motives are pure!
NET.2: But as for me, my feet almost slipped; my feet almost slid out from under me.
NET.3: For I envied those who are proud, as I observed the prosperity of the wicked.
NET.4: For they suffer no pain; their bodies are strong and well-fed.
NET.5: They are immune to the trouble common to men; they do not suffer as other men do.
NET.6: Arrogance is their necklace, and violence their clothing.
NET.7: Their prosperity causes them to do wrong; their thoughts are sinful.
NET.8: They mock and say evil things; they proudly threaten violence.
NET.9: They speak as if they rule in heaven, and lay claim to the earth.
NET.10: Therefore they have more than enough food to eat, and even suck up the water of the sea.
NET.11: They say, “How does God know what we do? Is the sovereign one aware of what goes on?”
NET.12: Take a good look! This is what the wicked are like, those who always have it so easy and get richer and richer.
NET.13: I concluded, “Surely in vain I have kept my motives pure and maintained a pure lifestyle.
NET.14: I suffer all day long, and am punished every morning.”
NET.15: If I had publicized these thoughts, I would have betrayed your loyal followers.
NET.16: When I tried to make sense of this, it was troubling to me.
NET.17: Then I entered the precincts of God’s temple, and understood the destiny of the wicked.
NET.18: Surely you put them in slippery places; you bring them down to ruin.
NET.19: How desolate they become in a mere moment! Terrifying judgments make their demise complete!
NET.20: They are like a dream after one wakes up. O Lord, when you awake you will despise them.
NET.21: Yes, my spirit was bitter, and my insides felt sharp pain.
NET.22: I was ignorant and lacked insight; I was as senseless as an animal before you.
NET.23: But I am continually with you; you hold my right hand.
NET.24: You guide me by your wise advice, and then you will lead me to a position of honor.
NET.25: Whom do I have in heaven but you? I desire no one but you on earth.
NET.26: My flesh and my heart may grow weak, but God always protects my heart and gives me stability.
NET.27: Yes, look! Those far from you die; you destroy everyone who is unfaithful to you.
NET.28: But as for me, God’s presence is all I need. I have made the sovereign LORD my shelter, as I declare all the things you have done.
